title: Karen Karniol-Tambour on Investing in a More Fragmented World
gist: Summary of a 2026 Milken panel where Bridgewater Co-CIO Karen Karniol-Tambour argues modern mercantilism is a structural inflationary force, most investors are overconcentrated in US equities and under-allocated to real assets, the AI boom is still in its buildout phase, and geographic fragmentation is creating distinct regional investable ecosystems.

insights.claim: Modern mercantilism is a structural rather than cyclical force, and is inherently inflationary because every country is spending to close its own vulnerabilities.
insights.speaker: Karen Karniol-Tambour
insights.so_what: Implies a persistent inflationary backdrop that argues against duration-heavy, deflation-era portfolio construction.
insights.quote: Modern mercantilism is a structural, not cyclical, force—every country spending to close its vulnerabilities is inherently inflationary.

insights.claim: Most investors are overweight US equities, underweight real assets, and poorly positioned for the environment ahead due to portfolio concentration.
insights.speaker: Karen Karniol-Tambour
insights.so_what: Argues for diversifying away from concentrated US equity exposure and adding real assets ahead of a higher-inflation regime.
insights.quote: Portfolio concentration leaves most investors overweight US equities, underweight real assets, and poorly positioned for the environment ahead.

insights.claim: The AI capex boom is driving US growth today, but markets are still living on the buildout phase rather than the adoption phase that follows.
insights.speaker: Karen Karniol-Tambour
insights.so_what: Flags a transition risk: when capex spending normalizes before adoption-driven productivity arrives, the growth and equity tailwind could falter.
insights.quote: The AI capex boom is driving US growth today, with markets still largely living on the buildout phase rather than the adoption phase that follows.
insights.locator: The AI capex boom is driving US growth

insights.claim: Geographic fragmentation is creating distinct investable ecosystems as industrial policy supports national champions, warranting exposure across regions.
insights.speaker: Karen Karniol-Tambour
insights.so_what: Supports multi-region allocation rather than a single global beta, as policy-favored national champions diverge across jurisdictions.
insights.quote: Geographic fragmentation is creating distinct investable ecosystems as industrial policy supports national champions, warranting exposure across regions.
